name: AST SpaceMobile, Inc.
business: AST SpaceMobile is building the first and only global Cellular Broadband network in space, accessible directly by unmodified smartphones for commercial use and for government applications. The service is planned to be provided by a constellation of high-powered, large phased-array satellites in LEO using low-band and mid-band spectrum controlled by mobile network operators. The company intends to work with MNOs to offer the service to end-users and also target government customers.

exposures.sentence: We are building the first and only global Cellular Broadband network in space to be accessible directly by everyday smartphones (2G/4G-LTE/5G devices) for commercial use, and for other applications for government use utilizing our extensive intellectual property ( IP ) and patent portfolio.

We also intend to leverage our patented technology, including the large phased array and high power capability of our BlueBird ( BB ) satellites, for a variety of non-communication and communication applications in the government sector.

In addition, when we introduce our own AST5000 Application Specific Integrated Circuit ( ASIC ) chip in the Block 2 BB satellites, we expect to achieve materially greater throughput capacity of up to 40 MHz per beam to continue to support 120 Mbps peak data rates and up to 10,000 MHz of processing bandwidth per Block 2 BB satellite, require less power and offer a lower overall unit cost.
The SpaceMobile Service currently is planned to be provided by a constellation of high-powered, large phased-array satellites in LEO using low-band and mid-band spectrum controlled by MNOs.

catalysts.event: Launch of five Block 1 BB satellites
catalysts.sentence: We launched five Block 1 BB satellites on September 12, 2024.

catalysts.event: First SpaceMobile video call with Vodafone
catalysts.sentence: In January 2025, we successfully made the first SpaceMobile video call from space with Vodafone using standard unmodified smartphones.
On July 21, 2025, we and AT&T made the first-ever Voice over LTE ( VoLTE ) call and short message service over satellite using AT&T s spectrum and core network with a standard unmodified cell phone.

catalysts.event: Canada first 4G VoLTE call with Bell Canada
catalysts.sentence: On October 2, 2025, together with Bell Canada, we achieved Canada s first-ever space-based 4G VoLTE voice call, broadband data connection, and video streaming using everyday smartphones.

On October 8, 2025, we announced the signing of a definitive commercial agreement with Verizon to provide direct-to-cellular AST SpaceMobile service when needed for Verizon customers within the continental United States (excluding Alaska) and Hawaii starting in 2026.

On October 29, 2025, we entered into a ten-year commercial agreement with Saudi Telecom Company ( STC ) to enable direct-to-device satellite mobile connectivity across Saudi Arabia and key regional markets.

catalysts.event: First $420.0 million payment to Ligado for Inmarsat
catalysts.sentence: On October 31, 2025 we made the first $420.0 million payment to Ligado for the benefit of Inmarsat.

catalysts.event: Launch of first Block 2 BB satellite (BB6)
catalysts.sentence: On December 23, 2025, we launched our first Block 2 BB satellite ( BB6 ), which features up to approximately 2,400 square feet phased array, the largest phased array ever deployed in a LEO for commercial use and more than three times larger than the phased array of the Block 1 BB satellites.

catalysts.event: Successful deployment of BB6 phased array
catalysts.sentence: On February 10, 2026, we successfully deployed BB6, the largest phased array deployed commercially in LEO.

catalysts.event: Planned launch of 45 to 60 Block 2 BB satellites by end of 2026
catalysts.sentence: We have commenced our launch campaign with the launch of BB6 on December 23, 2025 and plan to launch approximately 45 to 60 Block 2 BB satellites by the end of 2026, at a cadence of one launch approximately every one to two months on average.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 5.8 billion mobile subscribers constantly moving in and out of coverage, 3.4 billion people with no Cellular Broadband coverage, and 300 million people with no connectivity or mobile cellular coverage.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Groupe Speciale Mobile Association ( GSMA ), as of December 31, 2025, approximately 5.8 billion mobile subscribers are constantly moving in and out of coverage, approximately 3.4 billion people have no Cellular Broadband coverage and approximately 300.0 million people have no connectivity or mobile cellular coverage.
